headline: What role did France play in the American Revolution?
subhead: On July 4, Americans are celebrating the 250th anniversary of their independence – an event in which the French played a part.

Climate change: 'We cannot meet the challenge of adapting housing with the tools of the past. A thorough overhaul is needed'
subhead: Christophe Rodriguez, the director of the French Institute for Building Energy Performance, explains in an interview with Le Monde the urgent need to adapt institutions in order to carry out the 'project of the century' – the renovation of France's building stock.

headline: 'We sacrificed everything': In Nablus, the relentless decline of Palestinian society
subhead: In the occupied West Bank, Israel's financial stranglehold on the Palestinian Authority, combined with the proliferation of military checkpoints, has pushed society into unprecedented poverty. In Nablus, the economic heart of the occupied territory, all sectors have faced a sharp decline in living standards.

headline: Paul Seixas, the 19-year-old phenom carrying France's Tour hopes
subhead: For his first appearance at the Tour de France, which begins Saturday in Barcelona, the 19-year-old French cyclist is already seen as one of the top rivals to four-time winner Tadej Pogacar.

headline: To go or not? The dilemma for artists and authors invited to far-right-run cities
subhead: Many professionals in the French cultural sector are questioning whether to take part in events held in towns governed by the Rassemblement National, which is waging a cultural offensive.

subhead: The lessons from the UK's unprecedented vote to leave the European Union 10 years ago have been all too quickly forgotten, writes Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon.

headline: Four graphs to visualize the unprecedented scale of France's June heatwave
subhead: France's most recent heatwave began on June 17 and reached unprecedented levels in terms of both peak temperatures and official alert status. It proved to be more intense than the infamous 2003 heatwave and could also surpass its duration.

headline: Early heatwaves are devastating French agriculture and leaving farmers helpless
subhead: Scorched crops, decimated livestock and double-digit production drops: as farmers take stock of the effects of extreme heat, they fear the worst is yet to come.
